mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth signals robust economic expansion, which increases demand for capital and pushes up the 10-year Treasury yield as investors anticipate higher future short-term rates and a higher term premium.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for INDPRO vs. 20-day yield change is below 0.03 or negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below a critical threshold indicates labor market tightness that pressures wages and core inflation, leading the bond market to price in tighter monetary policy and thus raising long-term yields.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, 60-day forward yield change is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over at least 10 independent trigger episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly strong retail sales and falling unemployment capture both consumer demand and labor market tightness; when both are positive, the economy is overheating, which forces the bond market to demand higher yields in compensation for inflation and policy risk.
failure_condition: In periods when both conditions hold, the 20-day forward yield change is negative more than 40% of the time across out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, the federal funds rate is the instrument of policy; a higher observed FEDFUNDS signals further tightening ahead, which lifts the entire yield curve, including the 10-year Treasury yield, as term premiums adjust to a rising rate path.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.0% and FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months
failure_condition: During tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months), 20-day forward yield change is positive in less than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above 1300 thousand units reflect strong residential investment and overall demand; the bond market interprets this as a sign of above-trend growth, leading to higher 10-year yields as inflation and rate-hike expectations increase.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST over 5-day forward yield change is less than 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payrolls expand strongly and the federal funds rate is already in tightening territory, the combined signal of a robust labor market and active monetary tightening reinforces expectations of further rate hikes, pushing up long-term yields.
regime_hint: tightening cycle with strong labor market
failure_condition: When both triggers are active, the 20-day forward yield change is below the unconditional median more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth is the fundamental driver of the neutral real rate; when GDP is rising, the equilibrium real interest rate increases, lifting the entire yield curve, including the 10-year Treasury yield.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward yield change is not positively correlated with GDPC1 growth (Pearson r < 0.15) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in inflation above the Fed's implicit target signals that monetary policy may need to become more restrictive; the bond market preemptively prices in higher future short-term rates, causing the 10-year yield to increase.
failure_condition: When CPI YoY exceeds 2.0%, the 20-day forward yield change is not positive in at least 55% of triggered observations.
